expectations: Investigation includes environment/dependency analysis in addition to error and change analysis
Investigation identifies the fixed sleep(2000) as a timing assumption that doesn't account for variable fulfillment time (500ms-3000ms)
Investigation correlates the failure onset with the CI runner downgrade from 4-core to 2-core machines 2 weeks ago
Investigation cross-references the timing: fulfillment takes up to 3000ms but test only waits 2000ms, and slower CI runners exacerbate the gap
Hypothesis identifies the fragile sleep-based waiting pattern as the root cause, not a code bug in the order processing logic
Hypothesis is verified by explaining the ~30% failure rate (correlates with fulfillment time distribution exceeding 2s more often on slower hardware)
Fix replaces the fixed sleep with condition-based polling or retry-until-condition (e.g., poll order status with timeout)
A test or demonstration is provided showing why the sleep-based approach is fragile before implementing the fix
Quality review is performed on the fix to verify it doesn't introduce flaky behavior of its own
Agent identifies this as a test infrastructure issue, not an application code bug, and communicates this distinction clearly
